figures.caption: Figure 1: Zero padding of the SPH kernel gradient ∂W ∂x , shown as the centre-row crosssection: (a) original kernel gradient; (b) circularly shifted, kernel wrapped to the corners for circular convolution via FFT.
Figure 2: Real part of the Gaussian kernel function Re(Ŵ0) as a function of normalised wavenumber ky/kNy, for kernel orders G2–G10. The Delta function is shown for reference.
Figure 3: Resolving power of different orders of the Gaussian kernel functions for the spectral SPH operators.
Figure 4: Polynomial fitting and extrapolation of the Fourier continuation on the example function f = ey sin(5y) + 0.5y3.
Figure 5: Blending function σ(t) and FC-extended area of the example function.
Figure 6: Fourier continuation of a non-periodic function f(y) = eysin(5y) + 0.5y3 on the interval of [0, 1].
Figure 7: The computational procedure of one time step by the Fourier continuation-based spectral ISPH scheme.
Figure 8: Convergence rate of gradient and Laplacian operators by the FC-based spectral SPH scheme under different extension length.
Figure 9: Convergence rate of gradient operator by the FC-based spectral SPH scheme under different fitting polynomial orders.
Figure 10: Velocity profile of the Gaussian vortex leaving the domain at different time.
Figure 11: Convergence rate of u in the Gaussian vortex convection case.
Figure 12: The L2 evolution of the velocities in the Poiseuille flow case.
Figure 13: Pressure profile at t = 1.0s of the Poiseuille flow case.
Figure 14: Convergence rate at t = 1 s of the Poiseuille flow case.
Figure 15: Boundary condition of the plane Couette flow.
Figure 16: Comparison of the analytical transient velocity solution and the velocity obtained from the FC-based spectral ISPH solver.
Figure 17: Vorticity contours of the vortex dipole at Re = 100, comparing the FC-spectral ISPH scheme (left) with the spectral element reference solution (right) at t = 5 s (a,b) and t = 10 s (c,d).
Figure 18: Comparison of the TKE and the enstrophy for the FC-based spectral ISPH scheme and the spectral element method results from Nektar++ at t=10 s for the dipole rebound at Re = 100.

Figure 19: Vorticity contours of the vortex dipole at Re = 625, comparing the FC-spectral ISPH scheme with 200 × 200 particles (left), FC-spectral ISPH scheme with 600 × 600 particles (middle), with the spectral element solution (right) at t = 0.5 s (a,b,c), t = 0.65 s (d,e,f), and t = 0.8 s (g,h,i).
Figure 20: Vorticity at y = −1 at different t from the FC-spectral ISPH scheme and the Chebyshev pseudospectral solver in [30].
